pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh (line 11), export_result.sh (line 11), and verifier.py - consistently used as output destination for the segmentation mask
Verified in setup_task.sh (line 35) where reference image is created by opening Peppers sample and saving as PNG, also in export_result.sh (line 12) and verifier.py

Explicitly stated in task description: 'Your goal is to create a binary mask that isolates ONLY the green pepper'. Verified in verifier.py lines 100-104 which defines green pepper HSV range (H: 35-90, S: 40-255, V: 40-255)

Explicitly stated in task description: 'excluding the red/yellow peppers'. Verified in verifier.py lines 106-116 which defines red pepper HSV range (H: 0-15 and 160-180, S: 50-255, V: 50-255) and checks for red overlap exclusion
Verified in verifier.py line 136: if iou > 0.5 then grants 30 full points for 'Excellent Green Pepper IoU'. This threshold exactly matches the metadata claim.
Verified in verifier.py line 146: if red_overlap_ratio < 0.1 then grants 20 full points for 'Successfully excluded Red Pepper'. This threshold exactly matches the metadata claim.

pi_items.verified_value: Fiji/ImageJ built-in sample image: peppers.gif (standard test image from https://imagej.net/ij/images/)
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 34 opens Peppers sample using Fiji macro: run("Peppers (178K)"). Verified peppers.gif exists at https://imagej.net/ij/images/ as a standard 512x512 color test image with file size 159K

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the Peppers sample image, a real, well-known standard test image from ImageJ/Fiji (512x512 pixels, available at https://imagej.net/ij/images/peppers.gif). The task requires segmenting a green pepper from a color image containing red, green, and yellow peppers using color thresholding. All metadata claims are verified: the expected output path is /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/green_pepper_mask.png, the reference image is generated at /home/ga/.hidden/peppers_reference.png, the minimum Intersection over Union (IoU) threshold for green pepper detection is 0.5, and the maximum allowable red pepper overlap is 0.1 (10%). The verifier uses HSV color ranges to generate ground truth masks: green (H: 35-90) and red (H: 0-15, 160-180), validating the agent's binary segmentation mask against these criteria.
